results.name: Bucharest Botanical Garden
results.snippet: Established in 1860, displays a variety of plants from all over the world.

results.name: King Michael I Park

images.caption: Alley in Herăstrău Park in autumn
Monument to the Founders of the EU

results.snippet: The largest of several parks around man-made lakes on the Colentina River running through the city’s north and east side) houses the Village Museum, an open-air theater, various sports grounds, an amusement park and numerous restaurants and clubs.

results.name: Cișmigiu Gardens

images.caption: Statue of Mihai Eminescu on Writers' Alley (Aleea Scriitorilor)

results.snippet: This a lovely small park; it's the oldest in the city (designed 1845-1860).

results.name: Tineretului Park
results.snippet: It has a large multipurpose building (Sala Polivalenta) used for concerts, sporting events, exhibitions, etc., an amusement park for children, boat-rental, several restaurants and bars.

results.name: Parcul Bordei
results.snippet: The area of the park was public property until September 2003, when the General Council of Bucharest changed the status of the terrain to private property of the municipality and then gave the terrain to Costică Constanda, an entrepreneur who intends to build houses on it.

results.name: Grădina Icoanei

images.caption: The Bulandra Theatre with the Toma Caragiu stage, viewed from Grădina Icoanei

results.snippet: Grădina Icoanei ("Icon's Garden") is a small park in central Bucharest, situated not far away from Piața Romană and Bulevardul Magheru.

results.name: Parcul Floreasca
results.snippet: Oracle Tower or Floreasca Tower, is an office building located in the city of Bucharest, Romania.

results.name: Viilor road bus stop
results.snippet: Buses from Athens (several times per week, 16–20 hours travel time, tickets around ) arrive at stations along Viilor road; also from Istanbul (three-four buses per day, 12–14 hours travel time, tickets around ) arrive to here.
